summary: AI threat-hunting platform that turns security intel into durable detections.
editor_note: Threat detection gets sharper when the unit is a hunt, not another alert queue. Nebulock maps hypotheses to MITRE ATT&CK, reads EDR, IAM, cloud, network, and SaaS telemetry, builds a context graph, and produces findings plus deployable rules. Unlike SIEM copilots that summarize alerts, it starts from threat intel and asks whether your environment is exposed.

product_evidence.claim: The platform page says Nebulock hunts across EDR, IAM, cloud, network, and SaaS sources using a behavioral context graph.
Nebulock describes a /LOCK/ framework that maps hypotheses to MITRE ATT&CK, observes baselines, correlates signals, and emits findings.
The official surface links docs, Trust Center, and GitHub resources including an agentic threat-hunting framework.

market_context.primary_user: Security operations teams and CISOs trying to turn threat intelligence into proactive detection coverage.
market_context.differentiation: Nebulock centers the workflow on hunt hypotheses, context graphs, and deployable detection rules rather than alert summarization.
market_context.why_now: Security teams are flooded with tools and threat intel, making automated hunt execution more valuable than another alert surface.

risks.claim: The risk is that transparent AI hunting still needs enough clean telemetry integrations and analyst trust to replace manual investigation.
risks.basis: VentureDex editorial assessment based on official product evidence and funding-source review.
